author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXII. 34-36.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But when the Pharisees had heard that He had put the Sadducees to silence, they were gathered together; and one of them, which was a lawyer, asked Him a question, tempting Him, and saying, Master, which is the great commandment in the law?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again doth the evangelist express the cause, for which they ought to have held their peace, and marks their boldness by this also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How and in what way?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because when those others were put to silence, these again assail Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when they ought even for this to hold their peace, they strive to urge further their former endeavors,and put forward the lawyer, not desiring to learn, but making a trial of Him, and ask, “What is the first commandment?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since the first commandment was this, “Thou shalt love the Lord thy God,” thinking that He would afford them some handle, as though He would amend it, for the sake of showing that Himself too was God, they propose the question.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Indicating from what they were led to this; from having no charity, from pining with envy, from being seized by jealousy, He saith, “Thou shalt love the Lord thy God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is the first and great commandment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the second is like unto this, Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherefore “like unto this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because this makes the way for that, and by it is again established; “For every one that doeth evil hateth the light, neither cometh to the light;”and again, “The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what in consequence of this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “They are corrupt, and become abominable in their ways.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, “The love of money is the root of all evils; which while some coveted after they have erred from the faith;”and, “He that loveth me, will keep my commandment.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But His commandments, and the sum of them, are, “Thou shalt love the Lord thy God, and thy neighbor as thyself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If therefore to love God is to love one’s neighbor, “For if thou lovest me,” He saith, “O Peter, feed my sheep,”but to love one’s neighbor worketh a keeping of the commandments, with reason doth He say, “On these hang all the law and the prophets.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So therefore what He did before, this He doth here also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, that both there, when asked about the manner of the resurrection, He also taught a resurrection, instruct ing them beyond what they inquired; and here, being asked the first commandment, He rehearses the second also, which is not much inferior to that (for though second, it is like that), intimating to them, whence the question had arisen, that it was from hatred.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For charity envieth not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By this He shows Himself to be submissive both to the law and to the prophets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherefore doth Matthew say that he asked, tempting Him, but Mark the contrary?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For when Jesus,” he saith, “saw that he answered discreetly, He said unto him, Thou art not far from the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They are not contradicting each other, but indeed fully agreeing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he asked indeed, tempting, at the beginning, but being benefitted by the answer, was commended.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not at the beginning did He commend him, but when he had said, “That to love his neighbor is more than whole burnt sacrifices,” then He saith, “Thou art not far from the kingdom;” because he overlooked low things, and embraced the first principle of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed all those are for the sake of this, as well the Sabbath as the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not even so did He make His commendation perfect, but yet deficient.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For His saying, “Thou art not far off,” indicates that he is yet falling short, that he might seek after what was deficient.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if, when He said, “There is one God, and there is none other but He,” He commended him, wonder not, but by this too observe, how He answers according to the opinion of them that come unto Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For although men say ten thousand things about Christ unworthy of His glory, yet this at any rate they will not dare to say, that He is not God at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then doth He praise him that said, that beside the Father, there is no other God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not excepting Himself from being God; away with the thought; but since it was not yet time to disclose His Godhead, He suffers him to remain in the former doctrine, and praises him for knowing well the ancient principles, so as to make him fit for the doctrine of the New Testament, which He is bringing in its season.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides, the saying, “There is one God, and there is none other but He,” both in the Old Testament and everywhere, is spoken not to the rejection of the Son, but to make the distinction from idols.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that when praising this man also, who had thus spoken, He praises him in this mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then since He had answered, He asks also in turn, “What think ye of Christ, whose Son is He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They say unto Him, The Son of David.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See after how many miracles, after how many signs, after how many questions, after how great a display of His unanimity with the Father, as well in words, as in deeds; after having praised this man that said, that there is one God, He asks the question, that they may not be able to say, that He did miracles indeed, yet was an adversary to the law, and a foe to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, after so many things, He asks these questions, secretly leading them on to confess Him also to be God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the disciples He asked first what the others say, and then themselves; but these not so; for surely they would have said a deceiver, and a wicked one, as speaking all things without fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So for this cause He inquires for the opinion of these men themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since He was now about to go on to His passion, He sees forth the prophecy that plainly proclaims Him to be Lord; and not as having come to do this without occasion, nor as having made this His aim, but from a reasonable cause.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For having asked them first, since they answered not the truth concerning Him (for they said He was a mere man), to overthrow their mistaken opinion, He thus introduces David proclaiming His Godhead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they indeed supposed that He was a mere man, wherefore also they said, “the Son of David;”but He to correct this brings in the prophet witnessing to His being Lord, and the genuineness of His Sonship, and His equality in honor with His Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not even at this doth He stop, but in order to move them to fear, He adds what followeth also, saying, “Till I make Thine enemies Thy footstool;”that at least in this way He might gain them over.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that they may not say, that it was in flattery he so called Him, and that this was a human judgment, see what He saith, “How then doth David in spirit call Him Lord?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how submissively He introduces the sentence and judgment concerning Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: First, He had said, “What think ye?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whose Son is He?” so by a question to bring them to an answer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then since they said, “the Son of David,” He said not, “And yet David saith these things,” but again in this order of a question, “How then doth David in spirit call Him Lord?” in order that the sayings might not give offense to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore neither did He say, What think ye of me, but of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this reason the apostles also reasoned submissively, saying, “Let us speak freely of the Patriarch David, that he is both dead and buried.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He Himself too in like manner for this cause introduces the doctrine in the way of question and inference, saying, “How then doth David in spirit call Him Lord, saying, The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit Thou on my right hand, until I make Thy foes Thy footstool;”and again, “If David then call Him Lord, how is He then his Son,”not taking away the fact that He is his Son, away with the thought; for He would not then have reproved Peter for this,but to correct their secret thoughts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that when He saith, “How is He his Son?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He meaneth this, not so as ye say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they said, that He is Son only, and not also Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this after the testimony, and then submissively, “If David then call Him Lord, how is He his Son?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But, nevertheless, even when they had heard these things, they answered nothing, for neither did they wish to learn any of the things that were needful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He Himself addeth and saith, that “He is his Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or rather not even this very thing doth He say without support, but having taken the prophet with Him, because of His being exceedingly distrusted by them, and evil reported of amongst them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To which fact we ought to have especial regard, and if anything be said by Him that is lowly and submissive, not to be offended, for the cause is this, with many other things also, that He talks with them in condescension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore now also He delivers His doctrine in the manner of question and answer; but He darkly intimates even in this way His dignity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was not as much to be called Lord of the Jews, as of David.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark thou also, I pray thee, how seasonable it is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when He had said, “There is one Lord,” then He spake of Himself that He is Lord, and showed it by prophecy, no more by His works only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He showeth the Father Himself taking vengeance upon them in His behalf, for He saith, “Until I make Thine enemies Thy footstool,” and great unanimity even hereby on the part of Him that begat Him towards Himself, and honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And upon His reasonings with them He doth set this end high and great, and sufficient to close fast their mouths.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they were silent from thenceforth, not willingly, but from their having nothing to say; and they received so deadly a blow, as no longer to dare to attempt the same things any more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “no one,” it is said, “durst from that day forth ask Him any more questions.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this was no little advantage to the multitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also unto them doth He henceforth direct His word, having removed the wolves, and having repulsed their plots.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For those men gained nothing, taken captive by vainglory, and having fallen upon this terrible passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For terrible is this passion and many-headed, for some set their heart upon power for the sake of this, some on wealth, some on strength.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But proceeding in order it goes on unto almsgiving also, and fasting, and prayers, and teaching, and many are the heads of this monster.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But to be vainglorious indeed about those other things is nothing wonderful; but to be so about fasting and prayer, this is strange and lamentable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But that we may not again blame only, come and let us tell the means, by which we shall avoid this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Against whom shall we prepare to contend first, against those that are vainglorious of money, or those of dress, or those of places of power, or those of sciences, or those of art, or those of their person, or those of beauty, or those of ornaments, or those of cruelty, or those of humanity and almsgiving, or those of wickedness, or those of death, or those after death?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed, as I have said, this passion hath many links,and goes on beyond our life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such a one, it is said, is dead, and that he may be held in admiration, hath charged that such and such things be done; and therefore such a one is poor, such a one rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the grievous thing is this, that even of opposite things is it made up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Against whom then shall we stand, and let ourselves in array first?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For one and the same discourse suffices not against all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Will ye then that it be against them that are vainglorious about almsgiving?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To me at least it seems well; for exceedingly do I love this thing, and am pained at seeing it marred, and vainglory plotting against it, like a pandering nurse against some royal damsel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For she feeds her in deed, but for disgrace and mischief, prostituting her and commanding her to despise her father; but to deck herself to please unholy and often despicable men; and invests her with such a dress, as strangers wish, disgraceful, and dishonorable, not such as the father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Come now, then, let us take our aim against these; and let there be an almsgiving made in abundance for display to the multitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Surely then, first vainglory leads her out of her Father’s chamber.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas her Father requires not to appear so much as to the left hand,she displays her to the slaves, and to the vulgar, that have not even known her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou a harlot, and pander, casting her into the love of foolish men, that according as they require, so she may order herself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Dost thou desire to see how it renders such a soul not a harlot only, but insane also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Mark then her mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when she lets go heaven and runs after fugitives and menial slaves, pursuing through streets and lanes them that hate her, the ugly and deformed, them that are not willing so much as to look at her, them that, when she burns with love towards them, hate her, what can be more insane than this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For no one do the multitude hate so much, as those that want the glory they have to bestow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Countless accusations at least do they frame against them, and the result is the same, as if any one were to bring down a virgin daughter of the king from the royal throne, and to require her to prostitute herself to gladiators, who abhorred her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These then, as much as thou pursuest them, so much do they turn away from thee; but God, if thou seek the glory that cometh from Him, so much the more both draws thee unto Himself, and commends thee, and great is the reward He renders unto thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou art minded in another way also to discern the mischief thereof, when thou givest for display and ostentation, consider how great the sorrow that then comes upon thee, and how continual the desponding, while Christ’s voice is heard in thine ears, saying,“Thou hast lost all thy reward.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in every matter indeed vainglory is a bad thing, yet most of all in beneficence, for it is the utmost cruelty, making a show of the calamities of others, and all but upbraiding those in poverty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if to mention one’s own good actions is to upbraid, what dost thou think it is to publish them even to many others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then shall we escape the danger?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If we learn how to give alms, if we see after whose good report we are to seek.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me, who has the skill of almsgiving?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Plainly, it is God, who hath made known the thing, who best of all knows it, and practises it without limit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thou art learning to be a wrestler, to whom dost thou look? or to whom dost thou display thy doings in the wrestling school, to the seller of herbs, and of fish, or to the trainer?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet they are many, and he is one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then, if while he admires thee, others deride thee, wilt thou not with him deride them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What, if thou art learning to box, wilt thou not look in like manner to him who knows how to teach this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou art practising oratory, wilt thou not accept the praise of the teacher of rhetoric, and despise the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then is it other than absurd, in other arts to look to the teacher only, but here to do the contrary? although the loss be not equal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there, if you wrestle according to the opinion of the multitude, and not that of the teacher, the loss is in the wrestling; but here it is in eternal life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou art become like to God in giving alms; be thou then like Him in not making a display.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even He said, when healing, that they should tell no man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But dost thou desire to be called merciful amongst men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is the gain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The gain is nothing; but the loss infinite.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these very persons, whom thou callest to be witnesses, become robbers of thy treasures that are in the heavens; or rather not these, but ourselves, who spoil our own possessions, and scatter what we have laid up above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: O new calamity! this strange passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where moth corrupteth not, nor thief breaketh through, vainglory scattereth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is the moth of those treasures there; this the thief of our wealth in heaven; this steals away the riches that cannot be spoiled; this mars and corrupts all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because the devil saw that that place is impregnable to thieves and to the worm, and the other plots against them, he by vainglory steals away the wealth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But dost thou desire glory?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Doth not then that suffice thee which is given by the receiver himself, that from our gracious God, but dost thou set thine heart on that from men also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Take heed, lest thou undergo the contrary, lest some condemn thee as not showing mercy, but making a display, and seeking honor, as making a show of the calamities of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed the showing of mercy is a mystery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shut therefore the doors, that none may see what it is not pious to display.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For our mysteries too are above all things, a showing of God’s mercy and loving-kindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: According to His great mercy, He had mercy on us being disobedient.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the first prayer too is full of mercy, when we entreat for the energumens; and the second again, for others under penance seeking for much mercy; and the third also for ourselves, and this puts forward the innocent children of the people entreating God for mercy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since we condemn ourselves for sins, for them that have sinned much and deserve to be blamed we ourselves cry; but for ourselves the children; for the imitators of whose simplicity the kingdom of heaven is reserved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this image shows this, that they who are like those children, lowly and simple, these above all men are able to deliver the guilty by their prayers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the mystery itself, of how much mercy, of how much love to man it is full, the initiated know.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do thou then, when according to thy power thou art showing mercy to a man, shut the doors, let the object of thy mercy see it only; but if it be possible, not even he.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou set them open, thou art profanely exposing thy mystery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Consider that the very person, whose praise thou seekest, even himself will condemn thee; and if he be a friend, will accuse thee to himself; but if an enemy, he will deride thee unto others also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And thou wilt undergo the opposite of what thou desirest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thou indeed desirest that he should call thee the merciful man; but he will not call thee this, but the vainglorious, the man-pleaser, and other names far more grievous than these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou shouldest hide it, he will call thee all that is opposite to this; the merciful, the kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For God suffers it not to be hidden; but if thou conceal it, the other will make it known, and greater will be the admiration, and more abundant the gain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that even for this very object of being glorified, to make a display is against us; for with respect to the thing unto which we most hasten and press, as to this most especially is this thing against us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so far from obtaining the credit of being merciful, we obtain even the contrary, and besides this, great is the loss we undergo.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For every motive then let us abstain from this, and set our love on God’s praise alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus shall we both attain to honor here, and enjoy the eternal blessings, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might world without end.
